theory.intro: Rectangles with the same area can have different side lengths, and rectangles with the same perimeter can also have different shapes.
facts: Area $=$ length $\times$ width; many length/width pairs give the **same area**.
Perimeter $=$ $2 \times (\text{length} + \text{width})$; many pairs give the **same perimeter**.
Same area does **not** mean same perimeter, and the reverse is also true.
steps: List length/width pairs that multiply to the target area (or add to half the perimeter).
Compute the other measure (perimeter or area) for each pair.
Compare to see how the shape changes.
